chunk_summary: This section discusses admission thresholds for pediatric nutrition failure in chronic cardiorespiratory disease, focusing on stability, growth, and feeding safety, with reference to malnutrition indicators and child growth standards.
learning_objectives: Understand the criteria for admission thresholds in pediatric nutrition failure related to chronic cardiorespiratory disease.
Identify key indicators of pediatric malnutrition using ASPEN and WHO guidelines.
Evaluate feeding safety and growth stability as factors in determining nutrition intervention routes.
key_facts: Pediatric malnutrition is classified using ASPEN/Academy of Nutrition and Dietetics guidelines.
WHO child growth standards include weight velocity as a critical indicator of growth stability.
Feeding safety is a key determinant in deciding the escalation of nutrition support routes.
The Indian Academy of Pediatrics provides specific guidelines for managing heart failure in children.
Pediatric Cardiac Intensive Care Society offers nutrition guidelines for congenital heart patients.
reference_quote: Indicators for pediatric malnutrition (Academy of Nutrition and Dietetics/ASPEN) are critical for assessing nutrition failure in chronic diseases.
document_key_topics: Child growth standards and weight velocity
Feeding safety in chronic cardiorespiratory disease
Nutrition guidelines for congenital heart patients
